title: Nephrodium glabrum
intro: Nephrodium glabrum, also known as the glabrous maidenhair fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Nephrodium glabrum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Nephrodium glabrum is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies for the treatment of coughs, bronchitis, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Nephrodium glabrum is classified as a species of fern within the family Matoniaceae. It belongs to the genus Nephrodium, which is now often included in the genus Aleuritopteris or considered as a synonym of the genus Aleuritopteris.
